zh.lifeSummary: 當Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree於1548年3月27日出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾奧爾崔時，他的父親Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree28歲，母親Agnes Cunningham of Caprington32歲。他於1562年5月31日與Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1578年9月10日在英國蘇格蘭維托史爾享年30歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree于1548年3月27日出生在苏格兰艾斯爾奧爾崔时，他的父亲Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree28岁，母亲Agnes Cunningham of Caprington32岁。他于1562年5月31日与Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1578年9月10日在英国苏格兰維托史爾享年30岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

en.lifeSummary: When Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree was born on 27 March 1548, in Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree, was 28 and his mother, Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, was 32. He married Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland on 31 May 1562, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 10 September 1578, in Wigtownshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 30, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree est né le 27 mars 1548, à Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland le 31 mai 1562, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 10 septembre 1578, à Wigtownshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree am 27. März 1548 in Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree 28 und seine Mutter Agnes Cunningham of Caprington 32. Er hat am 31. Mai 1562 in Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 10. September 1578 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Wigtownshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree è nato il il 27 marzo 1548 nel Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland il 31 maggio 1562, nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 10 settembre 1578, nel Wigtownshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 30 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltreeが1548年3月27日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアオチルトレで生まれた時，父Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltreeは28歳，母Agnes Cunningham of Capringtonは32歳であった。1562年5月31日にイギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインでMargaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1578年9月10日にイギリススコットランドウィグトウンシュアイアで30歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree(이)가 1548년 3월 27일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 오치트리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Cunningham of Caprington(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1562년 5월 31일 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1578년 9월 10일 영국 스코틀랜드 위그타운실르에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.27 March 1548–10 September 1578: 27 de março de 1548–10 de setembro de 1578
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree nasceu em 27 de março de 1548, em Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland em 31 de maio de 1562, em Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de setembro de 1578, em Wigtownshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 30 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree родился 27 марта 1548 г. в местности очилтри, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland 31 мая 1562 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 10 сентября 1578 г. в местности вигтоуншир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.27 March 1548–10 September 1578: 27 de marzo de 1548–10 de septiembre de 1578
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Andrew Stewart Master of Ochiltree nació el 27 de marzo de 1548, en Ochiltree, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Andrew Stewart 2nd Lord Stewart of Ochiltree, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Agnes Cunningham of Caprington, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Margaret Stewart of Methven, relic of Ochiltree, latterly of Garthland el 31 de mayo de 1562, en Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 10 de septiembre de 1578, en Wigtownshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 30 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
